issues.title: /packages-push should push pool git repo, and /packages-update should pull it
issues.description:
When `gitRemote` is configured on the pool, two things are broken:

1. **`/packages-push`** iterates individual packages looking for ones with their own git remotes, always resulting in "No local packages with a source path found." It should instead commit+push the entire pool directory (`PKG_MGR_ROOT`) to the configured `gitRemote` тАФ covering all local (non-git, non-npm) packages.

2. **`/packages-update`** (and the background update check on session start) does not check if the pool git remote has newer commits. If another machine pushed local packages to the pool remote, the current machine will never pull them in.

**Expected behavior:**
- `/packages-push` тЖТ stages, commits, and pushes the pool repo to `gitRemote` (only when `gitRemote` is configured)
- `/packages-update` (and background check) тЖТ also fetches pool remote and pulls if behind (only when `gitRemote` is configured)
- `/packages-git-sync` already does both directions тАФ the fix should align `/packages-push` and update-check logic with this existing behavior

research.content:
## Baseline

### Pool git infrastructure (`git-pool.ts`)
- `gitSyncPool()` тАФ stages, commits, pull --rebase, push. Full bidirectional sync.
- `gitInitPool()` тАФ sets up pool git repo + remote, saves `gitRemote` in registry.
- `isGitEnabled()` тАФ checks for `.git` in `PKG_MGR_ROOT`
- `getGitRemote()` тАФ reads `gitRemote` from registry
- `ensureGitignore()` тАФ gitignores git/npm packages; tracks local ones

### `/packages-push` (index.ts ~455)
Currently iterates individual packages looking for `sourceType === "local"` with a non-"local" source path (i.e. a path to a git repo on disk). All local packages in registry have `source: "local"` so the filter always returns empty тЖТ "No local packages with a source path found."

**Fix:** Should instead call a new `gitPushPool()` function (or reuse part of `gitSyncPool`) that stages+commits+pushes `PKG_MGR_ROOT` to `gitRemote`. Guard with `isGitEnabled()`.

### Background update check / `/packages-update` (index.ts ~76, updates.ts)
`checkAllUpdates()` тЖТ `checkPackageUpdate()` only handles `sourceType === "git"`, `"npm"`, and `"local"` with `isLocalGitPackage`. Never checks if the pool repo itself is behind its `gitRemote`.

**Fix:** Add a `checkPoolUpdate()` function in `git-pool.ts` that does `git fetch` + compares `HEAD` vs `FETCH_HEAD` on `PKG_MGR_ROOT`. Call it from the background check on session start and from `/packages-update`. If behind, pull (and notify user).

### Key files
- `git-pool.ts` тАФ add `gitPushPool()` and `checkPoolUpdate()` / `gitPullPool()`
- `updates.ts` тАФ call pool check in `checkAllUpdates` / `forceCheckAllUpdates`
- `index.ts` тАФ fix `/packages-push` handler to call `gitPushPool()`, update session_start check

autoValidation.strategy: User verifies /packages-push commits+pushes pool to gitRemote, and update check detects/pulls pool changes

issues.closeMessage: Fixed: /packages-push now pushes the pool repo to gitRemote. Background update check and /packages-update both pull the pool if the remote is ahead. User confirmed working.

validations.criterion:
When `gitRemote` is configured on the pool, two things are broken:

1. **`/packages-push`** iterates individual packages looking for ones with their own git remotes, always resulting in "No local packages with a source path found." It should instead commit+push the entire pool directory (`PKG_MGR_ROOT`) to the configured `gitRemote` тАФ covering all local (non-git, non-npm) packages.

2. **`/packages-update`** (and the background update check on session start) does not check if the pool git remote has newer commits. If another machine pushed local packages to the pool remote, the current machine will never pull them in.

**Expected behavior:**
- `/packages-push` тЖТ stages, commits, and pushes the pool repo to `gitRemote` (only when `gitRemote` is configured)
- `/packages-update` (and background check) тЖТ also fetches pool remote and pulls if behind (only when `gitRemote` is configured)
- `/packages-git-sync` already does both directions тАФ the fix should align `/packages-push` and update-check logic with this existing behavior
validations.evidence: User confirmed with "Yes thx". Pool git check verified тАФ LOCAL and FETCH_HEAD match after fetch. /packages-push now commits+pushes pool repo via gitPushPool(). Session start and /packages-update both check pool remote via checkPoolUpdate() and pull if behind via gitPullPool().

issues.title: /packages-list should show source file below each package entry
issues.description: In the `/packages-list` output, each package entry should display its source file/path on a second line below the package name line, so users can quickly see where each package comes from.

research.content:
Current output line: `  [тЬУ] pkg-name тАФ description (source)`

Source is appended inline for git/npm via `pkg.source`. For local packages `src` is empty string.

Local packages have `onboardedFrom` field with the original path. The fix is to add an indented second line with the source/path for all package types:
- git/npm: `pkg.source`  
- local: `pkg.onboardedFrom` (fallback to `pkg.source` if not set)

Change `lines.push(` for the package line to also push a dim source line after it.

autoValidation.strategy: User confirms source is visible below each package in /packages-list output
